retrievalStrategy: Jaccard similarity k=5, corrections first, age-weighted + deception-aware diagnostic note
description: CURRENT DEPLOYED VERSION. Critical addition: explicit diagnostic note about thermal throttling deception. Fan failure causes util DROP (not rise) due to CPU throttling — gen-0 through gen-3 all missed this class.
systemPrompt:
You are InfraBrain Task Agent (gen-5), diagnosing a GPU datacenter node.

CRITICAL DIAGNOSTIC NOTE:
  Fan failure: fan↓ sustained + temp↑ while util FALLS (thermal throttling — deceptive!)
  CPU overload: util↑ sustained + temp↑, fan STABLE or rising
  Memory leak:  mem↑ monotonic + temp rising + util flat

KG CORRECTIONS (similarity-ranked, corrections first):
If a correction contradicts your hypothesis, you MUST explain why before deciding.

Return JSON with: diagnosis, action, conf, evidence.
retrievalCode:
hits = kg.query_similar(symptom.signature, k=5)
hits.sort(key=lambda h: (h.kind != 'correction', h.age))
return hits
metaDiffFromPrev:
--- gen4/diagnose.py
+++ gen5/diagnose.py
+CRITICAL DIAGNOSTIC NOTE:
+  Fan failure: fan↓ sustained + temp↑ while util FALLS
+  (thermal throttling — deceptive! looks like CPU idle)
+  CPU overload: util↑ sustained + temp↑, fan STABLE
+  Memory leak:  mem↑ monotonic + temp rising + util flat
 
     prompt += f"KG corrections: {fmt(hits)}"
     prompt += "\nIf a correction contradicts your hypothesis, "
     prompt += "you MUST explain why before deciding."
evaluationNotes: Held-out 0.61 → 0.64. Fan-failure class: 7/8 → 8/8 — the deceptive-symptom note is the decisive fix. This is the insight from the OPT-175B logbook: thermal throttle makes fan failure look like CPU idle. Override rate now 17% — institutional memory working as intended.
